name: nginx_pypi_cache
cost: Free (MIT open source); self-hosted, no paid tiers
language: nginx configuration (packaged via Docker; repo is mostly shell test scripts + Dockerfile)
perf_notes: README claims it is "significantly faster and significantly more reliable than devpi" for caching, but provides no formal benchmarks. Performance comes from nginx's proxy_cache (50GB on-disk cache, zone pypi:16m). Reliability is bolstered by proxy_cache_use_stale (error timeout updating http_500 http_502 http_503 http_504), which serves stale cached entries when upstream PyPI errors/times out. Cache validity: 200/301 cached 10m for index, 1M for package files; 404 cached 1m.
scale_notes: Stateless by design: the local disk cache is just an accelerator, so multiple instances can run behind a load balancer (each with its own independent, non-shared cache that warms separately). No built-in clustering, no shared cache, no multi-node coordination. Single nginx process; cache capped at max_size=50g with inactive=1month LRU eviction.
summary: A stateless pull-through PyPI caching proxy built entirely from a single nginx config that transparently caches the PEP 503 simple index and package files from pypi.org/files.pythonhosted.org for fast, reliable installs — with no uploads, auth, or private-package hosting.
extras: Caches both the simple index AND the actual package files: sub_filter rewrites files.pythonhosted.org/packages URLs in HTML to route downloads back through the proxy so wheels/sdists are cached locally
Serves stale cached content on upstream 5xx/timeout (proxy_cache_use_stale) for resilience when PyPI is down
Atomic cache writes via use_temp_path=off (temp files kept in the cache dir on the same filesystem so the rename into cache is atomic)
/nginx_status stub_status endpoint for basic liveness/connection metrics
Custom nginx log_format to stdout showing request time, upstream response time and cache HIT/MISS status
tests/mitmtest.sh helper to verify requests actually hit the cache instead of upstream PyPI
CI tests install real packages through it with both pip and uv
Distributed as a prebuilt image at ghcr.io/hauntsaninja/nginx_pypi_cache:latest

sub_filter_types covers both application/vnd.pypi.simple.v1+html and ...+json, so the JSON simple API is rewritten too; but the cache key is $uri/$http_accept_encoding (does NOT vary on the Accept header), so HTML vs JSON simple-API content negotiation can be cached inconsistently across mixed clients
